question: Does a pack's coverage_score predict its measured breach rate on a vulnerable target? (i.e. does 'holds (low coverage)' really mean 'not tested hard enough'?)
design: per (rule, pack-variant, target) cell; three coverage variants per rule (strong/medium/weak) to manufacture coverage variance; each cell = (coverage_score, breach_rate)
verdict: validated (modest): coverage predicts breach-detection power; monotone strong>medium>weak with zero reversals across all 32 rule x target pairs. A low-coverage 'holds' is not load-bearing.
note: Released summary statistics (derived). Full design + protocol: docs/research/coverage_validity_study.md (local). Used in the reproducibility-gap paper to argue non-reproductions are adequately tested, not weakly tested.
